title: NÚKIB portal now supports quantum-resistant cryptography
summary: Announcement by the Czech National Cyber and Information Security Agency that its portal now supports quantum-resistant cryptography, and setting out expectations for when post-quantum cryptography should be deployed for sensitive information of critical confidentiality in higher-risk environments.

title: The quantum threat and quantum-resistant cryptography (minimum requirements appendix)
summary: Official NÚKIB document (appendix to minimum requirements) explaining the quantum threat and quantum-resistant (post-quantum) cryptography, including the origins and definition of post-quantum asymmetric algorithms.

title: National Cyber Security Strategy 2026-2030
summary: Czech national cyber security strategy for 2026-2030, which identifies emerging disruptive technologies including artificial intelligence and post-quantum cryptography as means to strengthen cyber security.

title: Czech research mission to Taiwan strengthens cooperation in cybersecurity
summary: NÚKIB news item on a Czech research mission to Taiwan whose discussions covered quantum key distribution, post-quantum cryptography, and the use of artificial intelligence in cybersecurity. Flagged for review as the PQC reference is one topic in a diplomatic mission readout rather than the document's substance.

title: Analysis of investment options in further asset classes: Part I, digital assets
summary: Czech National Bank (central bank) research analysis on investment in digital assets which notes the quantum threat and discusses quantum-resistant signature schemes (post-quantum cryptography). Flagged for review as the PQC mention is incidental to a financial-research paper rather than a dedicated QSC document.

title: New European project QARC will ensure quantum-resistant data security
summary: NÚKIB news item (20 February 2026) announcing the Horizon Europe / ECCC-funded QARC ('Quantum-Resistant Cryptography in Practice') project, coordinated by Brno University of Technology, in which NÚKIB is a partner responsible for cryptographic expertise and coordinating the national transition to post-quantum cryptography, including developing and validating PQC implementation methodologies for critical state systems.

title: Minimum Requirements for Cryptographic Algorithms (v4.1)
summary: Latest edition (v4.1, dated 4 March 2026) of NÚKIB's official 'Minimum Requirements for Cryptographic Algorithms', which updates recommended algorithms in line with NIST's finalised post-quantum cryptography standards and supports preparation for the transition to quantum-resistant cryptography.

title: NÚKIB has updated the Minimum Requirements for Cryptographic Algorithms
summary: NÚKIB recommendation notice (February 2025) announcing the updated Minimum Requirements for Cryptographic Algorithms, including the addition of post-quantum algorithm names and parameters based on the final NIST PQC standards issued in August 2024 and support for preparing the transition to quantum-resistant cryptography.

title: NÚKIB prepared support materials for protection against the threat posed by quantum computers
summary: NÚKIB news item introducing three support materials explaining the quantum-computer threat to current cryptography and the steps required to defend against it, including the Minimum Requirements document that supports the transition to quantum-resistant (post-quantum) cryptography.

title: EU Member States warn of the quantum threat and call for the transition to post-quantum cryptography
summary: NÚKIB news item stating that it has joined the joint statement 'Securing Tomorrow, Today: Transitioning to Post-Quantum Cryptography' of 18 EU member states, agreeing with its conclusions and endorsing preparatory steps such as quantum-threat impact analysis and a risk-oriented PQC migration roadmap.

title: Kvantové technologie a komunikace (Quantum Technologies and Communications) – study for the Ministry of Industry and Trade
summary: Study (June 2025) prepared for the Czech Ministry of Industry and Trade under the National Recovery Plan, surveying quantum technologies and communications and containing a dedicated section (3.2) on post-quantum cryptography (PQC) / quantum-safe cryptography (QSC), its advantages and the migration challenge.
